markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define keyboard and command palette actions without relying on hidden chat context. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: EditorKeyboardAndCommandPalette leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EditorKeyboardAndCommandPalette is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 66669bce: editor_keymap.test.ts 6/6 - keyboard + command palette. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: H3 Monaco keystroke interception (+L15 isComposing guard)
evidence.fix: CodeBlockKeystrokeGuard PM plugin (priority 110) contains prose-level modifier chords originating inside the code-block host (StarterKit keymaps included) with palette as the global escape hatch, and single-owns Mod-Alt-c (inserts the EMBEDDED Monaco block; StarterKit's plain codeBlock bound the same chord). Component DOM listener gains defaultPrevented + origin + isComposing guards; fixes latent Mod-b/i/e double dispatch.
tests: RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: ignores editor chords that originate inside the embedded code block
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: contains StarterKit's native prose chords when they originate inside the code block
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: still opens the command palette (global chord) from inside the code block
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: keeps handling chords from the prose surface: Mod-Alt-c inserts an EMBEDDED Monaco block
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: keeps handling prompt-backed chords from the prose surface (Mod-k insert link)
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: does not double-dispatch chords already handled natively by ProseMirror

evidence.finding: M12 palette keyboard nav + M13 non-modal dialogs + L16 missing save binding
evidence.fix: ArrowUp/Down/Home/End + Enter roving option with combobox/listbox semantics (aria-activedescendant/aria-selected); focus-in + Tab trap + Escape close + focus restore for palette/arg-prompt/export dialogs; Mod-s SAVE_ACTION (global from code blocks, browser dialog suppressed) + onSaveRequested prop + palette Save entry wired to RichDocumentView's guarded save
tests: RichTextEditor.a11y.test.tsx: navigates the palette with ArrowDown/Up and runs the active option with Enter
RichTextEditor.a11y.test.tsx: restores focus to the opener when the palette closes
RichTextEditor.a11y.test.tsx: traps Tab inside the arg-prompt dialog and closes it with Escape
RichTextEditor.a11y.test.tsx: fires onSaveRequested for Mod-s from prose AND from inside the code block, always preventDefault
editor_keymap.test.ts: resolves Mod-s to the save action and classifies it global; IME composition guard

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: editor_keymap.test.ts 6/6 PASS; palette test PASS (with the uncaught jsdom dispatch error noted in MT-169)

findings.title: Editor keydown handler intercepts keystrokes bubbling out of the embedded Monaco editor - chords typed while editing code mutate the prose document
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:225-241 attaches the keymap handler to editor.view.dom, which CONTAINS every Monaco NodeView DOM subtree; Monaco does not stop propagation for keys it does not bind (Mod-b/i/e/k/p, Mod-Alt-c are all unbound in standalone Monaco). Typing Mod-Alt-c or Mod-k while focused in Monaco runs prose commands against the LAST ProseMirror selection - if that selection is the NodeSelection of the code block being edited (the normal state after clicking into it), insertContent REPLACES the code block: the operator loses the block they are typing in. Mod-p opens the prose palette over the code editor.
findings.fix: In the keydown handler, return early when event.target is inside [data-testid=monaco-code-block] (or when !editor.isFocused); add a test simulating a keydown from inside the Monaco host asserting no prose command runs.

findings.title: Command palette has no keyboard navigation or selection state
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:324-369 - input handles only Escape; no ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter handling; role=listbox with li[role=option aria-selected=false] wrapping inner buttons (invalid pattern - interactive content inside option, aria-selected never updates). A keyboard user must Tab through every row; VS Code palette semantics (arrows + Enter) absent.
findings.fix: Add active-index state: ArrowUp/Down moves, Enter runs, aria-activedescendant on the input, aria-selected reflects the active row; drop the inner buttons or switch to the combobox pattern.

findings.title: aria-modal dialogs are not modal: no focus trap, no focus restore, Escape only works from the input
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:324-406 palette + arg prompt declare aria-modal=true but Tab walks out of the dialog into the page behind it; closing does not restore focus to the invoking control; Escape is bound only on the palette input (not the arg prompt or dialog container).
findings.fix: Trap Tab within the dialog while open, bind Escape at the dialog container, and restore focus to the trigger on close (standard dialog pattern).

findings.title: resolveShortcut ignores IME composition state
findings.evidence: editor_keymap.ts:65-69 and the RichTextEditor handler never check event.isComposing; a chord-shaped keydown during active IME composition dispatches a command and preventDefaults mid-composition.
findings.fix: Return null/skip when event.isComposing (and key === "Process"); add a composition-guard unit test.

findings.title: No save binding and no save command in the palette
findings.evidence: EDITOR_KEY_BINDINGS (editor_keymap.ts:40-48) has 7 chords; Mod-s is absent and save lives outside the command catalog entirely (RichDocumentView button only) - for an editor-replacement surface, save is keyboard-unreachable and palette-invisible.
findings.fix: Add a doc.save command id wired by the host (RichDocumentView passes an onSave into the editor context) + Mod-s binding with preventDefault.

parity_gaps: ED-NAV-001 command palette: PARTIAL (full catalog wired, but no keyboard nav/selection)
ED-NAV-002 keymap: PARTIAL (7 chords; no save/undo listing/customization; ED-WB-009 keybindings UI unowned)
missing_tests: keydown originating inside the Monaco host does not trigger prose commands
palette ArrowDown/Enter runs the highlighted command
focus is trapped in palette/arg dialog and restored on close
chord during IME composition (isComposing=true) is ignored

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
